content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 7, 1988

Mark Neveau
Committee for Paramedics
in the Fire Service
2830 Barrington Terrace
Fremont, CA 94536

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-124

Dear Mr. Neveau:

You have requested advice regarding the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act of 1974.1/

QUESTION

Should the Committee for Paramedics in the Fire Service
file its recipient committee reports with the County of Alameda?

ANSWER

The Committee should file its recipient committee campaign
statements (Form 420) with the County of Alameda.

FACTS

The committee's statement of organization (Form 410)
indicates that its purpose is to promote the integration of
paramedics into local fire services within Alameda County.
Until recently, the committee has restricted the majority of
its activity to the City of Fremont. The committee now intends
to contribute to county candidates and officeholders, and other
candidates within the county.

Since its formation, the committee has filed its recipient
committee campaign statements (Form 420) with the city clerk of
the City of Fremont.

_____________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Neveau, Mark
Page Two

ANALYSIS

Section 82027.5(c) defines a county general purpose
recipient committee as one which supports or opposes candidates
or measures voted on in only one county, or in more than one
jurisdiction within one county. Since the committee now
intends to contribute to county supervisorial races and other
candidates within Alameda County, the committee must begin to
file as a county general purpose recipient committee.

Section 84215(d) specifies that a county general purpose
recipient committee must file the original and one copy of its
campaign statements with the clerk of the county.

If you have additional questions, please call me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

MR. KEVIN BRAATEN-MOEN
C/O FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814

3/21/88

DEAR MR. BRAATEN-MOEN:

MAY 24 9 4:02PM '88

AS PER OUR PHONE CONVERSATION ON 3/24/88 I AM REQUESTING THE F.P.P.C TO ISSUE
A LETTER TO THE POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE UNTIL FURTHER "NOTICE" FOR "ESTABLI-
IN THE FIRE SERVICE" ID #351549 INSTRUCTING US TO CHANGE OUR PRESENT FILING
AGENCY FROM THE CITY OF FREMONT TO THE COUNTY OF ALAMEDA.

THE PURPOSE OF THE ABOVE STATED COMMITTEE IS TO PROMOTE THE ACQUISITION OF
PARAMEDICS IN THE FIRE SERVICE. TO DATE THE MAJORITY OF OUR ACTIVITIES HAVE
BEEN CONFINED TO THE CITY OF FREMONT. WE HAVE BEEN SUCCESSUL WITH OUR PROPOSAL TO
THE CITY OF FREMONT, (THE FIRE DEPARTMENT NOW EMPLOYS PARAMEDICS). DUE TO
THIS WE FIND THAT THE MAJORITY OF OUR WORK IS CONDUCTED WITH THE COUNTY OF
ALAMEDA BECAUSE THE COUNTY IS THE GOVERNING AUTHORITY FOR FREMONT'S FIRE
PROGRAM AND NOT THE CITY OF FREMONT. WE HAVE ENDORSED ALAMEDA COUNTY SUPERVISOR
ED CAMPBELL INCUMBENT CANDIDACY FOR THE NOVEMBER ELECTION AND WE CONTEMPLATING
ENDORSING IN THE OTHER 2 ELECTIONS FOR ALAMEDA COUNTY SUPERVISORS' SEATS.

BASED UPON OUR ACTIVITIES WE FEEL TO FULLY COMPLY WITH THE FAIR POLITICAL
PRACTICES LAW WE SHOULD BE FILING WITH THE COUNTY OF ALAMEDA ON IN AN EFFORT

TO AVOID POSSIBLE FINES OF CONTROVERSY WE ARE REQUESTING A LETTER REITERATING
YOUR DECISION DURING OUR PHONE CONVERSATION.

IF YOU HAVE ANY QUESTIONS REGARDING THIS MATTER PLEASE GIVE ME A CALL AT
(415) 797-5693. THANK YOU IN ADVANCE FOR YOUR ASSISTANCE IN THIS MATTER.

CORDIALLY,

Mark Neveau

MARK NEVEAU
CHAIRMAN: COMMITTEE FOR PARAMEDICS IN THE FIRE SERVICE.
2830 BARRINGTON TERRACE
FREMONT, CALIFORNIA 94534

88-124

MELVYN BRAATEN-MOEN                                    3-21-88
C/O FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814

DEAR MR. BRAATEN-MOEN:

AS PER OUR PHONE CONVERSATION ON 3/14/88 I AM REQUESTING THE F.P.P.C TO ISSUE
A LETTER TO OUR POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE ENTITLED "COMMITTEE FOR PARAMEDICS
IN THE FIRE SERVICE" ASSISTING US TO CHANGE OUR PRESENT FILING
AGENCY FROM THE CITY OF FREMONT TO THE COUNTY OF ALAMEDA.

THE PURPOSE OF THE ABOVE STATED COMMITTEE IS TO PROMOTE THE ACQUISITION OF
PARAMEDICS IN THE FIRE SERVICE. TO DATE THE MAJORITY OF OUR ACTIVITIES HAVE
BEEN CONFINED TO THE CITY OF FREMONT. WE HAVE SUCCESSFULLY MET OUR GOAL FOR
THE CITY OF FREMONT, THE FIRE DEPARTMENT NOW EMPLOYS PARAMEDICS. DUE TO
THIS WE FIND THAT THE MAJORITY OF OUR WORK IS CONDUCTED WITH THE COUNTY OF
ALAMEDA BECAUSE THE "COUNTY" IS THE GOVERNING AUTHORITY FOR THE PARAMEDIC
PROGRAM AND NOT THE CITY OF FREMONT. WE HAVE ENDORSED ALAMEDA COUNTY SUPERVISOR
ED CAMPBELL INCUMBENT CANDIDACY FOR THE NOVEMBER ELECTION AND CONTEMPLATING
ENDORSING IN THE OTHER 2 ELECTIONS FOR ALAMEDA COUNTY SUPERVISORS' SEATS.

BASED UPON OUR ACTIVITIES WE FEEL TO FULLY COMPLY WITH THE FAIR POLITICAL
PRACTICES LAW WE SHOULD BE FILING WITH THE COUNTY OF ALAMEDA AND IN AN EFFORT

TO AVOID POSSIBLE FINES OR CONTROVERSY WE ARE REQUESTING A LETTER REITERATING
YOUR DECISION DURING OUR PHONE CONVERSATION.

IF YOU HAVE ANY QUESTIONS REGARDING THIS MATTER PLEASE GIVE ME A CALL AT
(415) 752-5509. THANK YOU IN ADVANCE FOR YOUR ASSISTANCE IN THIS MATTER.

CORDIALLY,

Mark Neveau

MARK NEVEAU
CHAIRMAN: COMMITTEE FOR PARAMEDICS IN THE FIRE SERVICE.
2830 BARRINGTON TERRACE
FREMONT, CALIFORNIA 94536

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 25, 1988


Mark Neveau
Committee for Paramedics
in the Fire Service
2830 Barrington Terrace
Fremont, CA 94536

Re:  88-124

Dear Mr. Neveau:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on March 24, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature] Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
